title: Make Crash Analytics settings searchable in the administration interface
author: atom-box
body: There are two Crash menus in the Matomo interface. Searching in the menu only shows one of them. This is confusing for users. To reproduce: 1. Open the Matomo interface >> Administration 2. Into the search bar over the navbar type "Crash" 3. There is only one result. The result you are looking for is hidden. After searching for "Crash" you can see (2) but must brute force view every menu to discover (3).

title: Improve Evolution Graph readability with trends
author: tsteur
body: Problem The current Evolution Graph accurately displays the underlying data, but it can be difficult to quickly understand whether a metric is improving, declining or simply fluctuating. I struggle with this personally quite often. This becomes even more challenging when: * viewing long time periods (12–24+ months), * displaying very large numbers (billions), (see also https://github.com/matomo-org/matomo/issues/24254) * comparing multiple metrics, * viewing the report with the left navigation visible or on smaller screens. The chart currently prioritises raw data over interpretation. Goals Make the Evolution Graph easier to interpret at a glance without reducing access to the underlying data. Potential improvements * Trend layer * Optional moving average (e.g. 3-month rolling average) * Visually distinguish actual values from the trend * Help users understand long-term direction rather than month-to-month noise * Make forecast and incomplete periods more clear Examples: Of course the screens need to be tweaked but are supposed to indicate what I mean. Not all the information we might have yet etc.

title: Support Industry-Standard Consent Mode Platform (CMP) Signals - t1
author: atom-box
body:
**Support Industry-Standard Consent Mode Platform (CMP) Signals**

Matomo should consume the existing Consent Mode signals rather than define a separate consent protocol. Consent Mode has become the de facto standard adopted by the major Consent Management Platforms (CMPs), allowing vendors to consume the same consent state without requiring CMP-specific integrations. Reusing this established protocol would allow Matomo to interoperate with existing CMP implementations while reducing the need for customers to build and maintain custom consent logic.

The consent layer should treat Consent Mode signals as the native source of truth. Rather than adapting `requireConsent` / `setConsentGiven` to Consent Mode, the tracker would listen directly to the `dataLayer` consent lifecycle (`default` and `update`) and adjust its behaviour accordingly. This would enable native hybrid tracking while aligning Matomo with the consent protocol already emitted by modern CMPs.

title: Feature request: Add support for AND in exclude_requests
author: gitressa
body:
The `exclude_requests` tool is great, but does not support fine grained rules, using multiple dimensions.

https://matomo.org/faq/how-to/how-do-i-exclude-specific-tracking-requests-in-matomo/


## Summary

The [`exclude_requests`](https://matomo.org/faq/how-to/how-do-i-exclude-specific-tracking-requests-in-matomo/) option is a great feature, but it does not support fine grained rules, since it only works on a single parameter ("OR") and may be too broad for many use cases:

> Multiple exclusions can be configured separated by a comma. The request will be excluded if any expressions matches (not all of them). For example: `"e_c==Media,action_name=@privacy"`. This would additionally exclude any request from being tracked where the page title contains privacy.

## Proposal

To only block relevant bots and not real visitors, you need to be fairly precise using "AND" to chain multiple dimensions, and only block if **all** expressions match. The currently supported "OR" delimiter is "," (comma) and perhaps we could add support for using "+" (plus) as "AND" delimiter, like this?

    exclude_requests = "resolution==1920x1080+entryPageUrl=@%2Fmypath+operatingSystemName==GNU%2FLinux"

See also:
- #https://github.com/matomo-org/plugin-TrackingSpamPrevention/issues/162

title: Incorrect documentation for increasing number of rows shown in Custom Reports
author: richardgaunt
body:
<!-- Please report only issues related to the documentation on https://matomo.org here -->
<!-- Documentation issues within our developer documentation on http://developer.matomo.org -->
<!-- should be reported at https://github.com/matomo-org/developer-documentation/issues/new -->

<!-- Please provide a short summary of the issue in the *Title* above -->

<!-- Please include the URL of the part of the documentation that should be fixed/improved -->
<!-- and describe which part causes you problems or the errors that are included in it -->

URL: https://matomo.org/faq/how-to/faq_54#custom-reports has reference to customising the number of rows shown in reporting and archiving including CustomReports

The variables shown in custom reports are no longer used (I can see them only being used in the 5.4.0 update for CustomReports.

This page should be updated to show the correct variables to be set for CustomReports - from what I can see it needs to be `datatable_archiving_maximum_rows_subtable_custom_reports_dimensions_1` `datatable_archiving_maximum_rows_subtable_custom_reports_dimensions_2` `datatable_archiving_maximum_rows_subtable_custom_reports_dimensions_3`

title: A/B testing: Add visitor-based conversion rate and unique conversion metrics
author: omsejla
body:
## Summary
In the A/B test report, the conversion rate is currently calculated based on visits. In many cases, this is not the best approach, as a visitor-based view would be more appropriate.

It would be useful to extend the report so users can choose whether the conversion rate should be calculated based on visits or visitors.

Ideally, goal conversions and ecommerce conversions should also have the option to be viewed as either unique events or total events.

For unique events, this would mean a 0–1 binary view per visitor, where the visitor either converted or did not convert. Total events would count all conversions or ecommerce events generated by the visitor.

For example, when running a cashier test, looking only at all conversions can produce skewed results depending on where more active users end up. A small number of highly active users can have a large impact on the results if all conversions are counted.

To reduce this “whale” impact, users often need to first look at unique conversions, meaning whether a visitor converted or not, and then as a second step look at orders or conversions per visitor.

This would make A/B test reporting more accurate and flexible, especially for cases where user-level performance is more relevant than visit-level performance.

## Your Environment
* Matomo Version:
* PHP Version: 8.4.22
* Server Operating System:
* Additionally installed plugins:

title: AB test report - allow segmentation with date
author: omsejla
body:
## Summary
During an A/B test, issues can sometimes be discovered only after the test has already finished. In these cases, it would be very useful to have a date filter or date segmentation available within the A/B test report.

This would allow users to exclude a specific timeframe where an issue was present, instead of having to treat the whole A/B test period as affected.

Example:
If an A/B test ran for a full month, but an implementation or tracking issue was present for only a few days, users should be able to filter out those dates and review the remaining valid test data.

This would help make A/B test reporting more accurate and reduce the need for manual workarounds or external calculations.

## Your Environment
* Matomo Version:
* PHP Version: 8.4.22
* Server Operating System:
* Additionally installed plugins:

title: Add configurable time window for the Real Time Map
author: jmumby
body: The Real Time Map currently displays the heading: **Real time visits in the last 24 hours** However, this is misleading because the map does not actually retain visits based on a 24-hour time window. Instead, the implementation retains the most recent N visits (default: 100). On low-traffic sites, this means the oldest pins displayed on the map may represent visits from several days or even a week ago, while the UI still indicates they occurred within the last 24 hours. **Proposed solution** Allow users to configure the time window used by the Real Time Map. For example, provide a selector to display visits from: Last hour Last 6 hours Last 24 hours Last 7 days Last 30 days Custom duration The map should only display visits whose timestamps fall within the selected time window. The heading should also be updated dynamically to reflect the selected period (e.g. "Real time visits in the last 7 days"). **Benefits** Makes the Real Time Map behaviour consistent with what users expect. Prevents the UI from displaying misleading information on lower-traffic sites. Gives users more flexibility to analyse recent visitor activity over an appropriate timeframe. Removes the dependency on a fixed number of retained visits, which produces inconsistent behaviour depending on site traffic. Additional context Currently, the retention of pins is determined by the number of recent visits retained rather than elapsed time. As a result, the same map may display only a few minutes of activity on a high-traffic site but several days or longer on a low-traffic site, despite always stating "Real time visits in the last 24 hours."

title: Feature request: Widget preview page
author: gitressa
body:
There are many great widgets, it's cumbersome to see what's available.

## Summary

I just discovered the awesome "Real-time map" widget by chance, clicking around under the different categories.


### Proposal

1. Give easy access to all widgets, and allow a user to quickly find interesting and relevant widgets by offering a "Preview Widget" page, where all widgets are shown.  
  
1. Widgets could be listed under their category:  
   - Visitors
   - Behaviour
   - Acquisition
   - Goals
   - AI Assistants
   - About Matomo
   - Insights
   - Marketplace

1. Allow adding multiple widgets to the Dashboard via a checkbox next to each widget, and clicking  "Add to Dashboard".

title: [Bug] reset password links don't preserve the path
author: janneman001
body:
### What happened?

Reset password works. However in a setup that contains a path component this is not honoured in the e-mails.

For example a setup like https://domain/path/... the links in the e-mail communication are: https://domain/....

Most end-users do not understand this and how to solve it.


### What should happen?

The expected behaviour is that matomo honours the path it is installed in. And sends links that contain the path as well.

### How can this be reproduced?

In a matomo setup that works with a path, like https://example.com/company1 when i reset the password i will get e-mails with a link like https://example.com/resetpasswordverylognumber. When i click on the link i get a 404 or other website

### Matomo version

I have seen this happing in matomo on-premise 5.7.1 as well as  5.10.x 

### PHP version

the php in matomo:5.7.1-apache docker image: PHP 8.4.18

### Server operating system

the os in matomo:5.7.1-apache docker image: Debian 13/ trixie

### What browsers are you seeing the problem on?

Firefox

### Computer operating system

OpenSuse 16.0

title: GDPR Tool feature request: Add support for custom maximum results, and show number of results
author: gitressa
body:
The GDPR Tool works well for deleting erroneously registered cloaked bot visits, but the hard coded maximum of 400 results can make a major clean up a tedious task.

## Summary

The GDPR Tool works well, and I am using it to delete cloaked bot visits, which are getting erroneously registered. However, I sometimes need to delete thousands of bot visits (5,000 is the record so far) after a long night bot attack. But there is a hard coded maximum of 400 visits:

> More than 400 results were found and the result was truncated to the first 400 visits.

... so I have to repeat the steps many times

### Proposal

**Custom number of results**

It would be nice if the hard coded maximum of 400 visits was made configurable, maybe via a "Maximum results" text input field, where "400"  is the default, but can be increased by the user?

A drop-down with options like these could also work:

- Select rows
- 100
- 200
- 400
- 1,000
- 2,000
- 5,000

**Display number of results**

To more easily check the consequence of adding another dimension as a filter, it would be nice if the number of results was always shown, something like this?

> ## Matching data subjects
> Results: 148 visits  <<<<< ADD THIS?
> These visits match the selected criteria. In case you [...]

title: [Bug] getLatestVersion API method
author: aqueos
body:
### What happened?

getLatestVersion API method

  Hi,

 thanks for your work. I have a bug to report concerning security of matomo upgrades. Well i dont know if you call this a bug or miss information or something else.

 If i ask getLatestVersion API method it returns a version but it does not tell you that the version returned can be an obsolete completely abandonned EOL version.


### What should happen?

 It would be decisive to have an api returning the status and eol date for each.

 To be secure, I am searching a way to know if my version is EOL or not, at the last version or not but the api that is for that seems to be only getLatestVersion and it can return v3  if your php is not upgraded.

  │ 3.14.0 │ 8.5    │ 4.16.2 │

3.314 tells me to go to v4 that is EOL.

  │ 3.14.0   │ 7.2 │ 3.14.1    │

if my php is old it even tells me to go to v3 as the lastest version :)

Si it is very hard to know how to report which version is out of date, wich is EOL etc.. nothing

Drupal is king here with a clear api with date so its easy to maintain a good healthy system that can give ideas 

https://updates.drupal.org/release-history/drupal/current
return xml with al infos

  Projet            : Drupal core
  Branches SUPPORTÉES (officiel) : 10.5, 10.6, 11.2, 11.3, 11.4
  Releases récentes :
    11.4.0-rc2   2026-06-17   SECURITY
    11.3.13      2026-06-23
    11.3.12      2026-06-17   SECURITY
    11.3.10      2026-05-20   SECURITY


It would be great to have a clear way to know the last version AND the status with date, it can be a simple static json build daily/when realease  by your system and cost no db/cpu call at all.

 hope that sparks ideas.

best regars,
Ghislain.


### How can this be reproduced?

try to automaticaly find if your version is not EOL.

### Matomo version

4

### PHP version

8.2

### Server operating system

debian

_No response_
